topics: Array, Hash Table, Linked List, Design, Hash Function
readme_description:
Design a HashSet without using any built-in hash table libraries.

Implement the `MyHashSet` class:

- `void add(key)` Inserts the value `key` into the HashSet.
- `bool contains(key)` Returns whether the value `key` exists in the HashSet or not.
- `void remove(key)` Removes the value `key` in the HashSet. If `key` does not exist in the HashSet, do nothing.

list.content:
```
Input
["MyHashSet", "add", "add", "contains", "contains", "add", "contains", "remove", "contains"]
[[], [1], [2], [1], [3], [2], [2], [2], [2]]
Output
[null, null, null, true, false, null, true, null, false]

Explanation
MyHashSet myHashSet = new MyHashSet();
myHashSet.add(1);      // set = [1]
myHashSet.add(2);      // set = [1, 2]
myHashSet.contains(1); // return True
myHashSet.contains(3); // return False, (not found)
myHashSet.add(2);      // set = [1, 2]
myHashSet.contains(2); // return True
myHashSet.remove(2);   // set = [1]
myHashSet.contains(2); // return False, (already removed)
```

readme_constraints:
- 0 <= key <= 10^6
- At most 10^4 calls will be made to `add`, `remove`, and `contains`.
helpers_assert_signature: (result: list[int | bool | None], expected: list[int | bool | None]) -> bool
test_imports:

from leetcode_py import logged_test

list.body:
result, _ = run_hash_set(MyHashSet, operations, inputs)
        assert_hash_set(result, expected)

inputs = [[], [1], [2], [1], [3], [2], [2], [2], [2]]
